title: WebSocket handler for managing connections and broadcasting messages
purpose: Handles WebSocket connections, manages subscriptions, and broadcasts messages to connected clients based on item IDs or channels.
module_context: Exports a WebSocketHandler class that maintains a set of active connections and manages agent command dispatching. Defines various message types for different project item updates and notifications.

steps.content: Store the provided agent dispatcher for later use in handling agent commands.
Initialize the WebSocket data with an empty set of subscriptions.
Add the WebSocket connection to the set of active connections.
Remove the WebSocket connection from the set of active connections.

methods.name: handleMessage
methods.normalized_params: (ws: ServerWebSocket<{ subscriptions: Set<string> }>, message: string): void

steps.content: Attempt to parse the incoming message as JSON.
IF the message is a subscription request, add the subscription ID or channel to the WebSocket's subscriptions.
ELSE IF the message is an unsubscription request, remove the subscription ID or channel from the WebSocket's subscriptions.
ELSE IF the message is an agent command, forward it to the agent dispatcher if one is registered.
IF an agent command is received but no dispatcher is registered, log an error.
IF parsing fails, log the error and continue processing other messages.
Convert the message to a JSON string for transmission.
Iterate through all active connections and attempt to send the message.
IF sending fails, track the connection as dead for cleanup.
Remove all dead connections from the active set to prevent memory leaks.
Convert the message to a JSON string for transmission.
Iterate through all active connections and check if they are subscribed to the specified diagram ID.
IF a connection is subscribed, attempt to send the message.
IF sending fails, track the connection as dead for cleanup.
Remove all dead connections from the active set to prevent memory leaks.
Convert the message to a JSON string for transmission.
Iterate through all active connections and check if they are subscribed to the specified document ID.
IF a connection is subscribed, attempt to send the message.
IF sending fails, track the connection as dead for cleanup.
Remove all dead connections from the active set to prevent memory leaks.
Convert the message to a JSON string for transmission.
Iterate through all active connections and check if they are subscribed to the specified spreadsheet ID.
IF a connection is subscribed, attempt to send the message.
IF sending fails, track the connection as dead for cleanup.
Remove all dead connections from the active set to prevent memory leaks.
Convert the message to a JSON string for transmission.
Iterate through all active connections and check if they are subscribed to the specified snippet ID.
IF a connection is subscribed, attempt to send the message.
IF sending fails, track the connection as dead for cleanup.
Remove all dead connections from the active set to prevent memory leaks.
Convert the message to a JSON string for transmission.
Generate a subscription key for the channel.
Iterate through all active connections and check if they are subscribed to the specified channel.
IF a connection is subscribed, attempt to send the message.
IF sending fails, track the connection as dead for cleanup.
Remove all dead connections from the active set to prevent memory leaks.
Construct a notification message using the provided data.
Convert the notification message to a JSON string for transmission.
Iterate through all active connections and attempt to send the notification.
IF sending fails, track the connection as dead for cleanup.
Remove all dead connections from the active set to prevent memory leaks.
Construct a status change message with the provided status and optional details.
Convert the status message to a JSON string for transmission.
Iterate through all active connections and attempt to send the status update.
IF sending fails, track the connection as dead for cleanup.
Remove all dead connections from the active set to prevent memory leaks.
Return the current count of active WebSocket connections.
